metadata.title: Fast, Scalable, Warm-Start Semidefinite Programming with Spectral Bundling and Sketching

sections.title: Motivation
content: Semidefinite programming (SDP) is an important tool for many applications
Many existing methods for solving or approximately solving SDPs
Need for a method that is fast, scalable, and provably correct for massive SDPs
Need a method where hyperparameters can modulate memory, accuracy, speed, and scalability of solving general SDPs

figures.description: Max-Cut graph visualization showing nodes and edges
Sparse matrix representation for matrix completion
Dense matrix representation for matrix completion
Vector field plot showing stability of linear dynamical systems
Polynomial optimization curve with local and global minima

sections.title: Problem Setup
content: max <C, X>s.t. A_i X = b_iA_i X ≤ b_iX ≥ 0
Want to solve general SDPs with both equality and inequality constraints
Want to have theoretical guarantees about convergence to optimal solution
Assume cost matrix C is sparse and a upper bound α ≥ tr(X*) exists
Assume the number of constraints is small compared to the problem size
Want to be able to solve SDPs where X will not fit in memory
Want solver to be able to take advantage of a warm-start initialization

figures.description: Geometric representation of SDP feasible region and objective function contours

sections.title: Unified Spectral Bundling
content: Reformulated SDP as a the following penalized dual objective:
f(y) := α [λ_max(C - A*y)]_+ + <b, y> + ι_y(y)
Solve the SDP by minimizing f using a proximal bundle method
The size of V determines the rank k of each update, specified by the user
Only update y_t+1 if progress is sufficient
Always use y_t+1 to create X_t+1, V_t+1 is the top-k eigenvectors of C - A*y_t+1
Analytical solution to the proximal bundle method lead to the following updates

figures.description: Mathematical decomposition of the spectral update directions in the bundle method

sections.title: Scaling with Matrix Sketching
content: Only need to keep track of projections of X such as <C, X> and AX
All of the memoization can be done efficiently due to linearity of operations
Apply low-rank matrix sketching techniques to enable scaling to problems where X cannot fit in memory — track projection P instead of X

figures.description: Matrix sketching process showing the projection of large matrices into lower dimensional space

Traveling Salesman Problem
Clustering with ∃-constraints
